description: Shades the region between a curve and the x-axis over a specified x-interval, representing the definite integral. Supports custom fill color and opacity for visualizing signed area and accumulation.
utility: Shade the area under a curve between two x-values

arguments.name: plotVariable
arguments.description: A previously assigned plot variable - e.g., f from f = plot(G, "x^2")

arguments.name: xmin
arguments.description: Left bound of the shaded interval

arguments.name: xmax
arguments.description: Right bound of the shaded interval

arguments.name: fillColor
arguments.description: Fill color using fc() expression - e.g., fc(blue), fc(red). Default is blue

arguments.name: fillOpacity
arguments.description: Fill opacity from 0 to 1 using fo() expression - e.g., fo(0.3). Default is 0.3

alternates.syntax:
f = plot(G, "f(x)")
areaunder(G, f, xmin, xmax)
alternates.description: Shade area under f(x) between xmin and xmax with default blue fill

alternates.syntax:
f = plot(G, "f(x)")
areaunder(G, f, xmin, xmax, fc(red))
alternates.description: Shade with a custom fill color

alternates.variant: With custom fill color and opacity
alternates.syntax:
f = plot(G, "f(x)")
areaunder(G, f, xmin, xmax, fc(green), fo(0.5))
alternates.description: Shade with custom fill color and opacity

examples.scenario: Area under x=sin(y) from 0 to pi with yellow fill and opacity
examples.expression:
g=g2d(at(4,21),20.4,17.7,range(-2*pi,2*pi,pi/2),range(-2,2,0.5),type(plotting))
p = plot(g, "x=sin(y)")
ar = areaunder(g, p, 0, 3.14, fc(yellow), fo(0.1))

examples.scenario: Area under x=sin(y) on a transposed-axis graph with yellow fill
examples.expression:
G=g2d(at(4,19),21.6,19.6,range(-2,2,0.5),range(-2*pi,2*pi,pi/2),type(plotting))
P = plot(G, "x=sin(y)")
ar = areaunder(G, P, 0, 3.14, fc(yellow), fo(0.2))

examples.scenario: Area under f(x) = 1+x squared from 0 to 2 with steelblue fill
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,6), 20, 20, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 8, 1), type(plotting), grid())
plot_2 = plot(graph_1, "1+x^2")
area_1 = areaunder(graph_1, plot_2, 0, 2, fc(steelblue))

examples.scenario: Strip area between x=2 and x=2.8 with red fill for derivative proof
examples.expression: area_2 = areaunder(graph_1, plot_2, 2, 2.8, c(orange), s(2), so(0.8), fc(red))

examples.scenario: Area under x² with outline and focus for emphasis
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(4,22), 17.2, 16.8, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting)); plot_1 = plot(graph_1, "x^2"); area_1=areaunder(graph_1,plot_1,-0.2,2.2); focus_1 = outline(desc_1, area_1)

examples.scenario: Two area-under regions on separate graphs for comparison
examples.expression: GA=g2d(at(6,2),16,16,range(-1,4,1),range(-1,8,1),type(plotting),grid()); fA = plot(GA, "1 + x^2", c(steelblue)); areaunder_1=areaunder(GA, fA, 0, 2, fc(steelblue), fo(0.3))
